TextENGNew: The moment club activities ended, Rena bolted home.
No doubt she had gone back to dig out that adorable Randy-kun doll she hadn't managed to carry home yesterday.
So today, for once, it was just the two of us—Mion and me—walking home together.

"A mountain of treasure, huh..."
"The people dumping their junk there probably never dreamed in a million years that Rena would be grateful to them."
"Mountain of treasure"—meaning that dam construction site we'd heard about.
...Right. Maybe Mion would tell me.
About the gruesome murder that was supposed to have happened there...

"Hey—that dam construction site over there."
"...Something happened there, didn't it?"
"A long time ago."

"Something sure did. A battle!"
"There were sit-ins, demonstrations—the whole works!"
Not quite what I'd been hoping to hear, but I decided to listen all the same.

"Our own land was going to be swallowed up by the reservoir."
"I'd have fought too."
Mion said so with a weary shrug, as if exasperated. Well, it's true enough—a garbage heap isn't exactly the kind of place a girl her age should be getting starry-eyed over...
changeReason: Old says Mion 'spoke excitedly' but JPN 呆れたようにやれやれと肩をすくめる means she shrugged wearily in exasperation — opposite emotion. Second half also omitted.

TextENGNew:
"But what kind of treasure could you possibly find in an illegal dumping ground?"
"I just hope she doesn't hurt herself."

"Ahahahaha!"
"She'll be fine, she'll be fine—it's not her first time."
"Besides, that place used to be the dam construction site, so there's all kinds of things lying around."
"All kinds of things... huh..."
changeReason: Old: 'Bustling...?' responding to a word Mion didn't say; new: 'All kinds of things... huh...' correctly echoing JPN 賑やか (lively/various things).

TextENGNew:
"Yeah, a lot happened there—battles and all that!"
"Sit-ins, demonstrations, you name it!"
"Battles" didn't sound like a peaceful word, but I decided to hear her out.
Mion launched into the story of those events, gesturing animatedly as she spoke.

"So they were going to drown Hinamizawa under the lake..."
"That really is unthinkable, when you put it that way."

"The government officials were absolutely one-sided about it."
"Arrogant and high-handed!"
"Once they realized money wouldn't settle the matter, they started harassing us with everything they had?!"
"Horrible people!"
Mion spoke with rising fury, as if the whole thing were playing out right before her eyes.
changeReason: Old says Mion 'spoke excitedly' but Japanese 声を荒げながら means 'raising her voice angrily/roughly' — wrong emotional register (excitement vs anger), though both are high-energy.

TextENGNew: "I'm amazed you actually won... You were up against the government, weren't you?"

"The village headman and the prominent families petitioned everyone they could reach."
"They went to Tokyo, worked their connections with all sorts of politicians..."
"...And in the end the plan was retracted."

"It was our total victory!"
"Ahahahahahaha!"

"There wasn't any... violence, was there?"
"Like assaults... or murder—"
changeReason: Old: 'murders' (plural); new: 'murder—' (singular, cut off). JPN 殺人 is singular, and the dash indicates Mion cuts him off.

TextENGNew: "There wasn't."
She said it flatly, cutting me off.
Just like Rena had.
Her words carried a period at the end—a full stop.
Tomitake-san had used the word "incident," and then said that something "hadn't been found."
changeReason: Old specifies 'one arm wasn't found' but JPN deliberately uses 何か (something) 'hadn't been found' — premature reveal spoiling narrative suspense.

TextENGNew: I'd been assuming there had been some kind of murder—that something had turned up missing... but maybe I'd been wrong.
changeReason: Old specifies 'murder by dismemberment' when JPN is deliberately vague (何か殺人事件 = some kind of murder); introduces specificity that prematures narrative details.

TextENGNew: My unsatisfied curiosity drooped inside my chest, restless and without anywhere to go.
A strange, nagging unease.
changeReason: Old: 'That's odd'; new: 'A strange, nagging unease.' JPN 奇妙な違和感 means 'a strange sense of dissonance/unease'—a feeling, not a casual observation.

TextENGNew: Her words carried a period—there was no opening for more.
Did she not want me prying any further? Or perhaps...
Perhaps this wasn't the kind of thing you could just bring up offhand and interrogate freely.
My unsatisfied curiosity drooped inside my chest, restless and without anywhere to go.

"Later then—see you tomorrow!"
"Kei-chan—don't wash that off until you're home, okay!?"
"I know, I won't!"
Mion stole glances at my face and struggled to hold back her laughter.
...I wonder just what sort of dreadful doodle they drew on me...
You'll regret this, Mion...!
If the tables ever turn, I'll cover every inch of your face—so thick that not even a scouring brush will get it off...!!!
I got home and ran straight into my mother, earning myself a fresh round of humiliation...
...If I could reason with certainty that the left card was not the joker, then I ought to have been able to declare that the right card was.
Even without Mion's goading—...the right was the most suspicious.

But wasn't it far too rash to decide based on Satoko's reaction alone?!
More carefully... slowly... Ah!

...I remember now...!!
That mark... the left card... there's no doubt!
The Seven of Clubs!!!

"Mmm..."
"I'm pretty sure that's the Seven of Clubs..."

I've got it!
The left card is the Seven of Clubs!
Which means the remaining right card is... the Joker!!!!
And then—just as my hand was about to touch the right card, I stopped dead.

"...Heh heh heh... heh heh heh..."
"Just what I'd expect from you, Mion."
The murmurs rippled through the room at my sudden pause—every last person had been certain the right card was the joker.

"?? Huh?"
"What on earth could Keiichi-san be saying??"
"But the left one is..."

"Oh...?"
"What makes you so certain the right one isn't the joker, Kei-chan?"
An unexpected remark from Mion.@k Everyone except me was thrown into confusion.

"I have no idea what the right card is."
"But what I do know is that the left card is the Seven of Clubs."

"Then!"
"Doesn't that leave the right card as the joker?!"
"One of the two has to be the joker—that was the deal!"

"Yeah."
"...One of the two is the joker—that much is certainly true."
"...Keiichi's intuition is sharp."

"Huh?"
"Rika-chan, what do you mean by...?"

"The thing is—"
"...the Seven of Clubs."
"...That's the card I discarded a little while ago when it paired with Satoko's Seven of Spades...!"
Everyone's gaze snapped to the discard pile in unison!
In that chaotic pile of discards, there was no way to tell truth from fiction...!

"In other words—"
"...Mion picked up one card from the discard pile... and layered it on top of the left... which means...!!!"

"...Oh! I understand!"
"The left card is being disguised as the Seven of Clubs, isn't it?!"
For the first time since the game began, a shadow crossed Mion's face—and I didn't miss it.
And then... with a dramatic half-spin and flourish, I made my decisive call!!
changeReason: Old: 'with a grand flourish'; new: 'with a dramatic half-spin and flourish.' JPN ３回転半のひねり means '3.5 rotations with a twist' (figure skating metaphor)—the spinning motion is meaningful.

TextENGNew: "The joker is... the LEFT haaaaaaaand!!!!!"
changeReason: Old: 'the left ooooooooooone'; new: 'the LEFT haaaaaaaand.' JPN 左手 specifically means 'left hand', not 'left one'.

TextENGNew:
A searing, white-hot moment...!
That single second felt like hours to everyone in the room.
The silence broke—and the first one to speak was... Mion.

"...As the founding club president, I've witnessed all manner of performances."
"...Kei-chan."
"...You are... the best."
"...The best of the best of the best!"
Probably the highest compliment Mion was capable of giving.@k She admitted defeat... and the cards slipped from both her hands.
...My... comeback victory!!!!!!
Rika-chan placed her hand gently on top of my head to celebrate my reversal victory.

"Huh...?"
"...Rika-chan... is...?"
Rena and Satoko were staring at us with wide, blank expressions... as though they'd seen a ghost.
"What is it, Rena? It's not like I cheated or anything, right?? Completely above board!"
"...Rika only pats someone on the head... when she's consoling them."
Huh...? What exactly was that supposed to mean...?
At that moment, Rena let out a short shriek.

"K-Keiichi-kun...!"
"...How can this be...!?!?"

"You know, Kei-chan."
"I thought you'd see through it that far."
"...Heheheheh!"
Everyone froze.@k ...Or was it just me...

"It was a gamble."
"If you'd jumped to conclusions like a little hothead, it would have been my loss."
changeReason: Old: 'like a little brat'; new: 'like a little hothead.' JPN おてんばさん means a tomboy/spirited girl, distinct from 'brat'.

TextENGNew: ...It was the answer everyone had been certain of...
Mion picked up the card from the losing hand.
"Kei-chan—you have every right to be proud of this loss."
She turned the card over...
...That card... was the one that declared the victor of this duel.
Hidden beneath the Seven of Clubs was... of all things, the Two of Diamonds!

I-I'd been outplayed at my own game...!!
If I'd just gone with the right one without overanalyzing it...!

"...In the name of club president Mion Sonozaki."
"...Keiichi Maebara."
"...I hereby grant your admission... to our club...!!!"
The moment I crumpled to my knees in a daze, everyone gave the game a thunderous round of applause.
changeReason: Old says applause was 'for the excellent effort I gave despite my defeat'; new says 'everyone gave the game a thunderous round of applause.' JPN この好ゲーム means 'this good game'—applause for the game, not for Keiichi personally.

TextENGNew:
"That settles it!!"
"Today's champion is me! Mion Sonozaki!! And our honorable last-place finisher is... Keiichi Maebara～!!!"
changeReason: Old: 'our exalted loser'; new: 'our honorable last-place finisher.' JPN ビリ specifically means last place/dead last, not just 'loser'.

TextENGNew: Everyone burst into gleeful cheering and applause.
The sting of defeat was real—but the cut was so clean and precise that it almost felt invigorating...
"When Mii-chan did that trick behind her back, I thought, 'She's going for it again～!' My heart was racing!"
"When Keiichi-san stopped right before touching the right card, I thought, 'He's fallen for it～!'"
"...He was caught quite splendidly."
...Huh?
Hold on a moment.
You all knew from the very start and were still that genuinely worked up...??
"...We all got excited together to make it more fun."
"You're all DEMONS!! You heartless monsters～!!!"

"Well then, time for Kei-chan's penalty...!"
"It is your first day in the club after all."
"I'll keep it gentle."
"...It would be awful if you stopped showing up to school because of this!"
The other three pinned down my arms and shoulders firmly.
Mion advanced, licking her lips...
Her right hand reached into her pocket to pull something out.
That's... a marker!! And not just any marker—a fat permanent one!!
"Now then... here we gooo～!"
Mion pulled the cap off the marker and inched its tip slowly, inexorably toward my helpless face...!
"N-No—st-stoooooop...o...o...!!!"
My dying wail echoed on and on...
